briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Define the LineMetadata data structure — holds per-line metadata: position IDs, dual hierarchy levels, and extensible attributes.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Line metadata: page/line IDs, tag hierarchy (reader-origin), result hierarchy (structure extractor), dynamic attributes. \par \b Input\/ \: Page ID, line ID, optional tag hierarchy level, optional result hierarchy level, extra kwargs. \par \b Output\/ \: LineMetadata instance convertible to API schema. \par \b Links\/ \: [INHERITS(5): Serializable, USES_API(8): ApiLineMetadata, READS_DATA_FROM(9): HierarchyLevel] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: tag_hierarchy_level is never None (defaults to create_unknown()) \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why two separate hierarchy levels (tag vs result)? A: Readers extract format-specific tags (tag_hierarchy_level) while structure extractors compute the final hierarchy (hierarchy_level). Preserving both enables debugging and fallback. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 – Added semantic markup and LDD logging] \par \b Module Map\/ \:
CLASS 10[Line metadata container] => LineMetadata METHOD 6[Constructor] => init METHOD 8[API schema conversion] => to_api_schema \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
